description: Add decoupled weight decay (0.01) to the Muon optimizer for matrix parameters. This shrinks the dynamic range of weight values during training, reducing outliers that waste int8 quantization range, which should narrow the gap between pre- and post-quantization BPB. The decay is applied only to matrix weights (the dominant artifact cost) and scales with the learning rate for proper warmdown behavior. (NO CHANGES)

Increase weight decay from 0.01 to 0.03 for Muon (matrix) parameters to reduce weight dynamic range and outliers during training, which should narrow the int8 quantization gap and improve post-quantization val_bpb. Higher decay shrinks weight magnitudes → smaller per-row quantization step sizes → better int8 fidelity at export time. (NO CHANGES)

Add straight-through int8 quantization-aware training (QAT) to CastedLinear forward pass. During each forward pass, weight matrices are simulated through per-row int8 quantize→dequantize with a straight-through estimator (gradients flow through the original weights). This teaches the model to learn weights that are robust to int8 rounding errors, reducing the gap between pre- and post-quantization val_bpb. Compute overhead is negligible (~0.01%) since the QAT ops (max, round, clip, multiply) are tiny compared to the matmul. The simulation matches the actual export quantization scheme (per-row int8 with symmetric scaling). (NO CHANGES)

Increase Muon weight decay from 0.03 to 0.06 to reduce weight magnitude outliers during training. Higher decay shrinks per-row dynamic range, yielding smaller int8 quantization step sizes and better post-quantization BPB fidelity. This targets the quantization gap directly — the raw pre-quantization BPB may be marginally higher, but the post-export BPB (the scored metric) should improve because weight distributions will be more int8-friendly. (NO CHANGES)

Change INT8_CLIP_PERCENTILE from 99.9 to 100.0 to align the export quantization scheme with the QAT simulation in CastedLinear. The QAT uses max(abs(w)) per row for scaling (no outlier clipping), but export was using the 99.9th percentile, creating a train-export mismatch. With QAT already teaching the model to minimize weight outliers, using the full max at export preserves the fidelity of the QAT-learned weight distribution and eliminates the quantization scheme discrepancy. (NO CHANGES)

Lower INT8_CLIP_PERCENTILE from 99.99984 to 99.5 to clip more weight outliers during quantization. This trades accuracy on the top 0.5% most extreme weights for better int8 precision across the remaining 99.5%, which should reduce post-quantization BPB because the quantization scale per row will be tighter and waste fewer levels on rare outlier values.

Add straight-through int8 quantization-aware training (QAT) to CastedLinear forward pass. During training, weights are simulated through per-row int8 quantize→dequantize with a straight-through estimator so gradients flow through original weights. This teaches the model to learn weights robust to int8 rounding, directly reducing the quantization gap (currently 0.0287). The QAT uses per-row amax scaling matching the export scheme. Compute overhead is negligible (~0.01%) since ops are element-wise and tiny compared to matmuls. Combined with the already-present Muon weight decay (0.02), this provides both magnitude regularization and quantization-noise robustness.
